alt: Archerfish leaping completely out of the water to grab an overhanging food item
caption: If an insect is perched low enough, an archerfish will bypass shooting altogether and leap completely out of the water to snatch the prey in mid-air.
acquisitionNotes: Curated fun-fact photograph illustrating remarkable aerial leaping adaptations. Local WebP ingested from https://upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/d/de/Toxotes_jaculatrix_%28banded_archerfish%29_2_%2815534430240%29.jpg
attributionText: Photo by James St. John via Wikimedia Commons, CC BY 2.0
